title: Information releases | Stats NZ
content:
## Information releases

## Births and deaths: Year ended March 2026
Births and deaths releases provide statistics on the number of births and deaths of people resident in New Zealand that are registered during a given period, along with selected fertility and mortality rates. They may differ from statistics presented elsewhere that relate to all births and deaths registered in New Zealand or to births and deaths occurring during a given period.

## Selected price indexes: April 2026
Selected price indexes (SPI) provide monthly price changes for a selection of goods and services that New Zealand households purchase.

## International migration: March 2026
International migration statistics give the latest outcomes-based measure of migration, which includes estimates of migrants entering or leaving New Zealand.

## International travel: March 2026
International travel covers the number and characteristics of overseas visitors and New Zealand resident travellers (short-term movements) entering or leaving New Zealand.

## Ready-mixed concrete: March 2026 quarter
Ready-mixed concrete statistics provide an indicator of construction activity.

## Livestock slaughtering statistics: March 2026
Livestock slaughtering statistics provide information about kills by region and animal type.

## Labour market statistics: March 2026 quarter
Labour market statistics provide a picture of the New Zealand labour market, including unemployment and employment rates, demand for labour, and changes in wages and salaries.

## Marriages, civil unions, and divorces: Year ended December 2025
Marriage and civil union statistics record the number of marriages and civil unions registered in New Zealand each year, and divorce statistics record the number of divorces granted in New Zealand each year.

## Building consents issued: March 2026
Building consent statistics are about the number, floor area, and value of new homes, non-residential buildings, and alterations approved for construction.

## Household living-costs price indexes: March 2026 quarter
Household living-costs price indexes (HLPIs) provide insights into the inflation experienced by 13 different household groups: beneficiaries, Māori, income quintiles (five groups), expenditure quintiles (five groups), and superannuitants. We publish an all-households HLPI for comparison.

## Business count indicators: March 2026
Business count indicators provide monthly counts of businesses grouped by industry and region.

## Employment indicators: March 2026
Employment indicators provide an early indication of changes in the labour market.
